perturbation.original_text: Distributor is and shall remain an independent contractor. Distributor agrees that STAAR has granted it no authority to act or make any representations or warranties on behalf of STAAR. Distributor is at all times acting for its own account, and at its own expense.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor is and shall remain an independent contractor. Distributor agrees that STAAR has granted it no authority to act or make any representations or warranties on behalf of STAAR. Distributor may sometimes act on behalf of STAAR, with STAAR's approval and expense.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the distributor 'may sometimes act on behalf of STAAR, with STAAR's approval and expense,' this introduces a contradiction to the classification of 'independent contractor.' An independent contractor generally operates independently and at their own expense. This creates ambiguity as to the true nature of the relationship and potential misclassification.
perturbation.contradicted_law: IRS definition of Independent Contractor vs. Employee
perturbation.law_explanation: The IRS uses a set of factors to determine whether a worker is an independent contractor or an employee. One key factor is control. If the company controls or directs the worker, the worker is more likely to be an employee. This modification suggests STAAR may exert control, contradicting the 'independent contractor' designation and potentially leading to tax and labor law issues.

Definitions (a) Wages For purposes of this chapter, the term "wages" means all remuneration for employment, including the cash value of all remuneration (including benefits) paid in any medium other than cash; except that such term shall not include- (1) in the case of the taxes imposed by sections 3101(a) and 3111(a) that part of th
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction because it allows the distributor to act on behalf of STAAR with STAAR's approval and expense, which undermines the 'independent contractor' designation. The Law Explanation states that the IRS uses control as a key factor in determining whether a worker is an independent contractor or an employee and this modification suggests STAAR may exert control. Furthermore, 26 USC 3121, defines wages as remuneration for employment, implying that if STAAR is covering the distributor's expenses while acting on its behalf, this could be viewed as an employer-employee relationship rather than an independent contractor relationship, thereby creating a potential contradiction with the 'independent contractor' classification in the original contract.

perturbation.original_text: STAAR warrants that, for the period of twelve (12) months from the date of delivery to Distributor hereunder (the "Warranty Period"), the Products will meet STAAR's published specifications or labeling for such Products as in effect at the time of such delivery ("Warranty"). To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law and except as otherwise stated in this Agreement: (a) the foregoing Warranty is exclusive; and (b) all other terms, representations, undertakings, rights, remedies and warranties are excluded, whether express or implied (including but not limited to any warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose or against infringement).
perturbation.changed_text: STAAR warrants that, for the period of twelve (12) months from the date of delivery to Distributor hereunder (the "Warranty Period"), the Products will meet STAAR's published specifications or labeling for such Products as in effect at the time of such delivery ("Warranty"). This warranty is offered at STAAR's discretion, and STAAR reserves the right to modify or revoke it at any time. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law and except as otherwise stated in this Agreement: (a) the foregoing Warranty is exclusive; and (b) all other terms, representations, undertakings, rights, remedies and warranties are excluded, whether express or implied (including but not limited to any warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose or against infringement).
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'This warranty is offered at STAAR's discretion, and STAAR reserves the right to modify or revoke it at any time,' the original warranty is undermined. The idea of a warranty is that it guarantees to provide some protection or recourse if issues occur with the purchased product. Adding the change allows STAAR to effectively negate the warranty it provides.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) Implied Warranty of Merchantability
perturbation.law_citation: UCC § 2-314 - Implied Warranty: Merchantability; Usage of Trade
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-314 implies a warranty of merchantability in contracts for the sale of goods, meaning the goods must be fit for their ordinary purpose. While this warranty can be disclaimed, the disclaimer must be conspicuous and unambiguous. Adding a clause allowing STAAR to revoke or modify the warranty at any time creates ambiguity and potentially renders the disclaimer ineffective, thus contradicting the UCC's requirements for a valid disclaimer of the implied warranty of merchantability.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-314. Implied Warranty: Merchantability; Usage of Trade. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-314. Implied Warranty: Merchantability; Usage of Trade. (1) Unless excluded or modified (Section 2-316 ), a warranty that the goods shall be merchantable is implied in a contract for their sale if the seller is a merchant with respect to goods of that kind. Under this section the serving for value of food or drink to be consumed either on the premises or elsewhere is a sale. (2) Goods to be merchantable must be at least such as (a) pass without objection in the trade under the contract description; and (b) in the case of fungible goods , are of fair average quality within the description; and (c) are fit for the ordinary purposes for which such goods are used; and (d) run, within the variations permitted by the agreement , of even kind, quality and quantity within each unit and among all units involved; and (e) are adequately contained, packaged, and labeled as the agreement may require; and (f) conform to the promise or affirmations of fact made on the container or label if any. (3) Unless excluded or modified (Section 2-316 ) other implied warranties may arise from course of dealing or usage of trade. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a clause allowing STAAR to revoke or modify the warranty at any time. This directly contradicts the implied warranty of merchantability under UCC § 2-314, as it creates ambiguity about the enforceability of the warranty. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing UCC § 2-314, states that a warranty of merchantability is implied unless excluded or modified under Section 2-316. The added clause undermines the warranty by allowing STAAR to unilaterally change or revoke it, potentially rendering any disclaimer ineffective and contradicting the UCC's requirements for a valid disclaimer. The modification effectively negates the implied warranty, making the goods potentially not fit for their ordinary purpose, which is a direct violation of the warranty.

perturbation.original_text: Except in a case where the relief sought is an injunction or other equitable relief, the parties agree that any dispute between the parties arising out of, relating to or in connection with this Agreement, whether characterized or sounding in contract or tort or otherwise, shall be resolved exclusively through binding arbitration conducted in accordance with the Rules of Conciliation and Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. The arbitration shall be held in Los Angeles, California, USA. Judgment upon the award rendered may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. Each party shall bear its own expenses of the arbitration, but the arbitration fees and costs shall be borne equally between the parties participating in the arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: Except in a case where the relief sought is an injunction or other equitable relief, the parties agree that any dispute between the parties arising out of, relating to or in connection with this Agreement, whether characterized or sounding in contract or tort or otherwise, shall be resolved exclusively through binding arbitration conducted in accordance with the Rules of Conciliation and Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. However, STAAR retains the right to pursue legal action in any court of its choosing. The arbitration shall be held in Los Angeles, California, USA. Judgment upon the award rendered may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. Each party shall bear its own expenses of the arbitration, but the arbitration fees and costs shall be borne equally between the parties participating in the arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'However, STAAR retains the right to pursue legal action in any court of its choosing,' the binding arbitration clause is contradicted. A binding arbitration clause means that both parties agree to resolve disputes through arbitration, not through court litigation. This change creates ambiguity and potentially renders the arbitration clause unenforceable.
perturbation.law_citation: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate
perturbation.law_explanation: The FAA establishes that arbitration agreements are 'valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract.' By retaining the right to pursue legal action, STAAR undermines the 'irrevocable' nature of the agreement to arbitrate, potentially rendering the clause unenforceable under the FAA.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: U.S.C. Title 9 - ARBITRATION 9 U.S.C. United States Code, 2011 Edition Title 9 - ARBITRATION CHAPTER 1 - GENERAL PROVISIONS Sec. 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate From the U.S. Government Publishing Office, www.gpo.gov §2. Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670.) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, §2, 43 Stat. 883.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the changed text, 'However, STAAR retains the right to pursue legal action in any court of its choosing,' directly contradicts the original clause mandating binding arbitration for dispute resolution. The FAA, as highlighted in both scraped law snippets (9 U.S. Code § 2), emphasizes that agreements to arbitrate are 'valid, irrevocable, and enforceable.' By allowing STAAR to pursue legal action in court, the changed text undermines the 'irrevocable' nature of the arbitration agreement, creating a legal contradiction and rendering the original arbitration clause potentially unenforceable. This falls under the Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction type, as the term 'binding arbitration' is misaligned by giving one party the right to pursue court litigation.
